QF.id: VI Functions of DP Account Operations_6

question.text: An individual can have how many BSDAs across all depositories?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Individuals shall have only one BSDA in their name across all depositories. **The Trap:** 'One per depository' would mean two BSDAs (one NSDL, one CDSL), which is not allowed. **Cross-Exam Context:** This single-account restriction is unique to BSDA. Regular demat accounts have no such limit. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The person must be the sole or first holder in the BSDA. This prevents individuals from splitting holdings across multiple BSDAs to avoid AMC.
